name: Service Station Dealers
description_short: Service Station Dealers Federal Credit Union is a small, NCUA-insured credit union headquartered in Philadelphia, PA, offering savings, CDs, mortgages, and loan products to members.
description_long:
Service Station Dealers Federal Credit Union was established in 1973 and operates as a federally chartered credit union insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). Based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, it serves a specialized membership traditionally connected to the service station industry. The credit union has remained small and focused, with approximately $0.13 million in assets as of 2026.

The institution offers a range of standard banking products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), mortgages, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, and personal loans. According to their profile on BestCashCow, they advertise competitive rates on select products, including a 1-Year CD at 4.59% APY, 30-Year Mortgages at 4.91%, and a HELOC at 0.99%. They maintain a single headquarters location at 906 Rhawn Street in Philadelphia.

What distinguishes Service Station Dealers is its niche positioning as a credit union historically serving service station operators and employees. As a traditional credit union, membership eligibility may be restricted to specific occupational or community groups rather than being open to the general public. This specialized focus differentiates it from larger, more accessible financial institutions and neobanks.

A significant caveat is that current rate and product information is incomplete on BestCashCow. The website explicitly states "We do not yet have deposit rates" and "There are currently no loan rates available," suggesting the profile data may be outdated or incomplete. With only $0.13 million in assets, this is an extremely small institution with very limited reach and capacity. Prospective members should contact the credit union directly to verify current rates, eligibility requirements, and available services.
pros: NCUA-insured deposits provide federal protection up to $250,000 per account
Established credit union with 50+ years of history since 1973
Offers competitive rates on select products (1-Year CD at 4.59% APY advertised)
Provides comprehensive banking products including mortgages, HELOCs, auto and personal loans
Credit union structure typically offers member-owned governance and potential for higher dividend rates
Specialized focus serves niche membership community effectively
cons: Extremely small asset base ($0.13 million) severely limits resources, services, and branch availability
Current rate information incomplete on major financial comparison sites; difficult to verify actual offerings
Membership likely restricted to specific occupational groups (service station dealers), not open to general public
Very limited online presence and outdated profile information suggests minimal digital banking infrastructure
Single location in Philadelphia with no branch network visibility, limiting accessibility
best_for: Current or retired service station dealers and employees seeking specialized credit union membership
Philadelphia-area residents with existing relationships to the service station industry
Members seeking traditional credit union benefits with community-focused lending
diagnosis: Service Station Dealers Federal Credit Union is best suited for current or former service station industry professionals in the Philadelphia area who qualify for membership and seek traditional credit union banking services. However, prospective members should be aware that the institution's extremely small asset base, limited digital presence, and incomplete rate information on major financial platforms make it difficult to assess current offerings and accessibility—direct contact with the credit union is essential before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
